topics.name: 2 Thessalonians
topics.description: Posts on 2 Thessalonians, including authorship, forgery, apocalyptic expectation, further reading, and later interpretation.

topics.name: Academic Life
topics.description: Posts on graduate study, teaching, dissertations, university life, scholarly careers, and what academic biblical scholars actually do.

topics.name: Academic Research and Publishing
topics.description: Posts on scholarly research projects, academic publishing, peer review, journals, reference works, editorial work, and the professional production of scholarship.

topics.name: Acts
topics.description: Posts on the book of Acts, including its literary features, historical reliability, major topics, theology, and account of early Christianity's spread.

topics.name: Afterlife (General)
topics.description: Posts on broad questions about life after death, including death, consciousness, reincarnation, animals, religious belief, and changing ideas of the afterlife.

topics.name: Agnosticism
topics.description: Posts on agnosticism, including Bart's own views, interviews, personal reflections, uncertainty about God, and the difference between agnosticism and atheism.

topics.name: Ancient Jewish Afterlife
topics.description: Posts on Jewish views of death and life after death, including Sheol, Ecclesiastes, Samuel, Enoch, resurrection, and debates among ancient Jewish groups.

topics.name: Ancient Literacy
topics.description: Posts on reading, writing, literacy rates, education, secretaries, and whether figures such as Jesus, Peter, or early Christians could read or write.

topics.description: Posts on ancient figures associated with miracle-working, including Vespasian, Jewish miracle workers, Jesus as a wonder-worker, and early Christian apologetic claims.

topics.description: Posts on ancient secretarial practices and authorship claims, including whether secretaries could compose texts, explain differences in style, or account for writings attributed to apostles.

topics.name: Ancient Wealth Ethics
topics.description: Posts on ancient views of wealth, poverty, contentment, charity, Cynic poverty, and moral arguments about possessions.

topics.description: Posts on traditions about the deaths of Peter, Paul, and other apostles, including martyrdom claims and what those stories are used to argue.

topics.description: Posts on archaeological evidence and material remains relevant to Jesus, early Judaism, and early Christianity, including Nazareth, Capernaum, crucifixion nails, artifacts, and the limits of archaeological claims.

topics.description: Posts on Arius, the Arian controversy, and debates over Christ's divine status around the Council of Nicaea.

topics.name: Athanasius

topics.description: Posts on Jesus' death as atonement, sacrifice, forgiveness, vicarious suffering, or the means of salvation.

topics.name: Augustine
topics.description: Posts on Augustine's writings, theology, conversion, views of miracles, ethics, and influence on later Christianity.

topics.name: Autobiographical Posts
topics.description: Posts about Bart's life, education, career, family memories, retirement, and personal experiences.

topics.description: Posts on biblical passages, translation issues, webinars, and interpretive debates about same-sex relations and sexual morality.

topics.name: Bible Translations (General)
topics.description: Posts on Bible translations broadly, including choosing translations, comparing versions, the Apocrypha, and general questions about how translations relate to manuscripts and interpretation.

topics.description: Posts on contradictions in biblical texts, including the Gospels, the Hebrew Bible, and broader questions of interpretation and scriptural authority.

topics.name: Biblical Inerrancy
topics.description: Posts on claims that the Bible is without error, including fundamentalism, contradictions, personal deconversion, and debates over biblical authority.

topics.name: Biblical Numerology
topics.description: Posts on number symbolism and numerical interpretation in biblical texts, especially 666 and Matthew's genealogy.

topics.name: Biblical Views of Suffering
topics.description: Posts on how biblical texts explain suffering, including divine punishment, wisdom traditions, Job, Ecclesiastes, and competing biblical answers.

topics.name: Charity

topics.description: Posts on Jesus' crucifixion as a historical event, including how it is reconstructed, disputed, interpreted, or compared with later religious claims.

topics.description: Posts on Greek New Testament manuscripts, early papyri, manuscript evidence, amulets, copying, and what manuscripts can or cannot show about original texts.

topics.name: Non-Canonical Gospels (General)
topics.description: Posts on early Christian gospels outside the New Testament, including their contents, historical value, theological claims, discovery, and reasons for exclusion from the canon.

topics.name: Non-Canonical Miracle Stories
topics.description: Posts on miracle stories in non-canonical Christian texts, including Apocryphal Acts, Infancy Gospel of Thomas, Acts of Thomas, and stories about the young Jesus or apostles.

topics.name: Non-Pauline Epistle Forgeries
topics.description: Posts on forged or pseudepigraphic non-Pauline letters, especially James, Jude, and the Johannine letters.

topics.name: Non-Pauline Epistles
topics.description: Posts on Hebrews, James, 1-2 Peter, the Johannine letters, Jude, and other New Testament letters not attributed to Paul.

topics.name: Non-Pauline Epistles Authorship
topics.description: Posts on authorship questions in Hebrews, Jude, 1 Peter, the Johannine letters, and other New Testament epistles not attributed to Paul.

topics.name: NRSV
topics.description: Posts on the New Revised Standard Version, including Bart's work on the translation committee, inclusive language, translation decisions, and the role of Bruce Metzger.

topics.name: Old Testament Apocrypha
topics.description: Posts on Jewish apocryphal writings outside the Protestant Old Testament, including Tobit, Judith, Sirach, Wisdom of Solomon, 1-2 Maccabees, additions to Daniel, and related texts.

topics.name: Oral Tradition
topics.description: Posts on the oral transmission of stories about Jesus, including storytelling, oral cultures, Papias, Gospel traditions before writing, and how traditions changed before becoming texts.

topics.name: Original Text
topics.description: Posts on whether the original wording of New Testament writings can be identified, including lost originals, authorial drafts, copied texts, and arguments for reconstruction.

topics.name: Orthodoxy and Heresy
topics.description: Posts on how early Christians defined orthodoxy and heresy, including competing Christian views, Bauer's thesis, Eusebius, Marcion, and Jewish-Christian groups.

topics.name: Otherworldly Journeys
topics.description: Posts on ancient journeys to heaven, hell, Hades, or the realm of the dead, including Enoch, Paul, the Apocalypse of Peter, the Apocalypse of Paul, and Greco-Roman traditions.

topics.name: Papias
topics.description: Posts on Papias, especially his claims about Gospel origins, oral tradition, Matthew, Mark, and early Christian testimony.

topics.name: Papyrus Egerton
topics.description: Posts on Papyrus Egerton, an early non-canonical gospel fragment, including its stories, relationship to other Gospel traditions, and significance for early Gospel circulation.

topics.name: Passion Narratives
topics.description: Posts on Gospel traditions about Jesus' final days, including the Last Supper, temple curtain, triumphal entry, blasphemy charge, Pilate, anti-Judaism, and narrative shaping.

topics.name: Patristic Textual Evidence
topics.description: Posts on using quotations in early Christian writers to reconstruct the New Testament text, including the limits, dating, location, and reliability of patristic evidence.

topics.name: Paul and Crucifixion
topics.description: Posts on how Paul interprets Jesus' crucifixion, including the crucified messiah, Jesus' death, and the meaning of the cross in Paul's letters.

topics.name: Paul and Jewish Law
topics.description: Posts on Paul's views of the Jewish law, including Galatians, Gentile believers, salvation, love, and debates over law observance.

topics.name: Paul and Opponents
topics.description: Posts on conflicts involving Paul, including rival teachers, disputes with other apostles, and later arguments about Paul's authority.

topics.name: Paul and Persecution
topics.description: Posts on Paul as persecutor and persecuted figure, including Acts, Paul's letters, historical reconstruction, and claims about opposition to Paul.

topics.name: Paul and Resurrection
topics.description: Posts on Paul's teaching about resurrection, including Jesus' resurrection, the resurrection of believers, spiritual bodies, and eternal life.

topics.name: Paul and Women
topics.description: Posts on Paul's views of women, women associated with Paul, women apostles, interpolation debates, and gender roles in Pauline communities.

topics.name: Paul in Acts
topics.description: Posts comparing the Paul of Acts with Paul's letters, including chronology, missionary activity, speeches, conversion stories, and historical reliability.

topics.name: Paul's Knowledge of Jesus
topics.description: Posts on what Paul appears to know about the historical Jesus, including Jesus' teachings, death, disciples, family, and traditions Paul cites.

topics.name: Pauline Apocalypticism
topics.description: Posts on Paul's end-time expectations, including Jesus' return, resurrection, the Spirit, the delay of the end, and later rapture interpretations of Paul's letters.

topics.name: Pauline Authorship
topics.description: Posts on which letters Paul wrote, which letters are disputed, and how scholars use style, theology, historical setting, and comparison with undisputed letters to assess authorship.

topics.name: Pauline Biography
topics.description: Posts on Paul's life and career, including conversion, travel, chronology, imprisonment, letters, and the major sources for reconstructing his biography.

topics.name: Pauline Communities
topics.description: Posts on the churches connected with Paul, including Corinth, Thessalonica, Gentile inclusion, community conflicts, and expectations about the end.

topics.name: Pauline Conversion
topics.description: Posts on Paul's conversion, its historical circumstances, and how it shaped his mission, theology, and understanding of Gentile inclusion.

topics.name: Pauline Ethics
topics.description: Posts on Paul's ethical teachings, especially slavery, Philemon, Onesimus, and moral questions in Pauline letters.

topics.name: Pauline Forgeries
topics.description: Posts on letters falsely attributed to Paul, including disputed Pauline letters, pseudepigraphy, motives for forgery, and debates over authenticity.

topics.name: Pauline Letter Composition
topics.description: Posts on how Pauline letters were composed, edited, combined, or transmitted, especially questions about 2 Corinthians and Philippians.

topics.name: Pauline Letters
topics.description: Posts on Paul's letters as a group, including lost letters, individual epistles, interpretation, communities, and broader questions about Paul's correspondence.

topics.name: Pauline Salvation Models
topics.description: Posts on Paul's understandings of salvation, especially judicial and participationist models, grace, atonement, and the believer's relation to Christ.

topics.name: Pauline Textual Criticism
topics.description: Posts on textual problems in Paul's letters, including variants, interpolations, scribal changes, manuscript evidence, and questions about the original text.

topics.name: Pentateuch
topics.description: Posts on the Torah/Pentateuch, including Moses, Exodus, Sinai, the law, contradictions, sources, and historical questions about its narratives.

topics.name: Persecution (General)
topics.description: Posts on persecution as a broad topic, including opposition to Christians, pagan criticism, suffering, resurrection hope, and Christian claims about Jesus.

topics.name: Persecution in the Epistles
topics.description: Posts on persecution, suffering, opposition, and endurance as addressed in New Testament letters such as 1 Peter, Hebrews, and 1 Thessalonians.

topics.name: Personal Reflections
topics.description: Posts offering personal reflections on belief, doubt, ethics, suffering, public issues, holidays, or life experience.

topics.name: Petrine Forgeries
topics.description: Posts on writings attributed to Peter, including debates over 1 Peter, 2 Peter, secretaries, literacy, and other texts falsely or questionably connected with Peter.

topics.name: Philippians
topics.description: Posts on Philippians, including authorship, composition, textual problems, further reading, and the letter's place among Paul's writings.

topics.name: Philippians Christ Poem
topics.description: Posts on Philippians 2:6-11, including Christ's status, humility, exaltation, and possible preexistence or incarnation.

topics.name: Pontius Pilate
topics.description: Posts on Pontius Pilate, including his role in Jesus' trial and crucifixion, Gospel portrayals, anti-Jewish traditions, burial questions, and later legends about Pilate.

topics.name: Popularizing Scholarship
topics.description: Posts on explaining biblical scholarship to wider audiences through articles, books, media, public responses, and accessible presentations of academic work.

topics.name: Problem of Suffering
topics.description: Posts on suffering as a challenge to belief in a loving and all-powerful God, including debates, proposed answers, apologetic arguments, biblical responses, and personal reflections.

topics.name: Prophets
topics.description: Posts on Hebrew Bible prophets, including Isaiah, Hosea, Zechariah, Daniel, prophecy, suffering servant traditions, and prophetic interpretation.

topics.name: Proto-Gospel of James
topics.description: Posts on the Proto-Gospel of James, including traditions about Mary's background, Jesus' birth, Jesus' brothers, and later Christian infancy stories.

topics.name: Purgatory
topics.description: Posts on purgatory and related ideas, including postmortem purification, unforgivable sin, degrees of punishment, Perpetua, and the historical emergence of purgatorial belief.

topics.name: Q Source
topics.description: Posts on Q as a proposed source behind material shared by Matthew and Luke, including its reconstruction, possible contents, and significance for the Synoptic Problem.

topics.name: Quran Fragments
topics.description: Posts on ancient Quran fragments, including discovery claims, radiocarbon dating, manuscript dating, and broader issues in comparing Quranic and biblical manuscript evidence.

topics.name: Redaction Criticism
topics.description: Posts on redaction criticism, especially how Gospel writers shaped, edited, and interpreted inherited traditions for their own theological or literary purposes.

topics.name: Resurrection Apologetics
topics.description: Posts on arguments for or against proving Jesus' resurrection, including debate claims, legal arguments, miracle reasoning, eyewitness claims, and apologetic defenses.

topics.name: Resurrection Appearances
topics.description: Posts on traditions that Jesus appeared after death, including appearances to disciples, women, Paul, the Twelve, the five hundred, and questions about visions or hallucinations.

topics.name: Resurrection of Jesus
topics.description: Posts on Jesus' resurrection broadly, including historical reconstruction, early Christian belief, miracle claims, tomb traditions, and how the resurrection was understood.

topics.name: Resurrection of the Dead
topics.description: Posts on future bodily resurrection, including Jewish origins, Daniel, Maccabean martyrdom, Paul, physical embodiment, personal identity, and resurrection as afterlife hope.

topics.name: Resurrection Theology
topics.description: Posts on theological interpretations of Jesus' resurrection, including messiahship, salvation, the beginning of the church, Isaiah 53, Trinity, and early Christian belief.

topics.name: Revelation
topics.description: Posts on the book of Revelation as an ancient apocalypse, including its authorship, historical setting, symbolism, violence, judgment, 666, afterlife imagery, and modern misreadings.

topics.name: Revelation Authorship
topics.description: Posts on who wrote Revelation, including the identity of John of Patmos and whether Revelation was written by the same author as the Gospel of John.

topics.name: Rich and Poor in Afterlife
topics.description: Posts on afterlife stories about wealth, poverty, reversal of fortunes, Lazarus and the rich man, and moral warnings about riches after death.

topics.name: Rise of Christianity
topics.description: Posts on how Christianity spread and became successful in the Roman world, including social, religious, political, and cultural explanations.

topics.name: Roman Crucifixion and Burial Practices
topics.description: Posts on Roman crucifixion and burial practices, including how crucifixion was carried out, whether victims were buried, and how this evidence bears on Jesus' burial.

topics.name: Roman Persecution
topics.description: Posts on Roman persecution of Christians, including imperial involvement, charges against Christians, Constantine, Roman religion, and the political context of persecution.

topics.name: Roman World
topics.description: Posts on Roman political, legal, military, and cultural settings relevant to Jesus, Paul, early Christianity, and the spread of the Christian movement.

topics.name: Salvation
topics.description: Posts on who is saved, how salvation happens, whether salvation can be lost, and how Jesus, Paul, or later Christians understood eternal life.

topics.name: Scholar Memorials
topics.description: Posts remembering scholars and public religious figures, including their work, influence, and significance for biblical or early Christian studies.

topics.name: Scribal Changes
topics.description: Posts on how scribes altered manuscripts, including accidental mistakes, intentional changes, theological edits, harmonizations, and the historical significance of copying.

topics.name: Septuagint
topics.description: Posts on the Greek translation of Jewish scripture, including its origin, textual issues, translation traditions, and importance for early Jewish and Christian interpretation.

topics.name: Sethian Gnostics
topics.description: Posts on Sethian Gnostics, including their myths, teachings, relationship to the Gospel of Judas, and place within discussions of Gnostic Christianity.

topics.name: Sexual and Reproductive Ethics
topics.description: Posts on biblical and modern debates about sexuality, reproduction, surrogacy, gender identity, premarital sex, and the status of the unborn.

topics.name: Signs in John
topics.description: Posts on the signs in the Gospel of John, including how John portrays Jesus' miracles, how John differs from the Synoptics, and whether signs function as evidence for Jesus' identity.

topics.name: Social Class in Antiquity
topics.description: Posts on class, status, labor, poverty, slavery, gender hierarchy, and social conditions in the ancient Mediterranean world.

topics.name: Son of Man
topics.description: Posts on the Son of Man sayings, including what the phrase meant, whether Jesus used it for himself, and its role in apocalyptic traditions.

topics.name: Source Criticism
topics.description: Posts on source criticism, including how scholars identify written sources, composite texts, Gospel relationships, and possible spliced or edited writings.

topics.name: Suffering and Loss of Faith
topics.description: Posts on suffering as a reason for losing or questioning faith, including personal reflections, disasters, miracles, and the problem of reconciling suffering with Christian belief.

topics.name: Synoptic Problem
topics.description: Posts on the literary relationships among Matthew, Mark, and Luke, including Markan priority, Q, source theories, and explanations for similarities and differences.

topics.name: Teaching and Courses
topics.description: Posts about teaching, courses, lectures, class assignments, online events, and ways biblical studies is presented to students or public audiences.

topics.name: Textbooks
topics.description: Posts about textbooks and introductory teaching materials, including how they are written, revised, illustrated, and used to teach biblical studies.

topics.name: Textual Criticism Methods
topics.description: Posts on how textual critics evaluate readings, weigh evidence, identify variants, reconstruct texts, and debate the aims and limits of the discipline.

topics.name: Textual Criticism Overview
topics.description: Posts introducing textual criticism as a field, including why textual variants matter, how textual scholars work, and common questions about the reliability of the New Testament text.

topics.name: Textual Variants
topics.description: Posts on differences among manuscript copies, why variants matter, and how changed wording can affect interpretation of biblical texts.

topics.name: Theological Textual Variants
topics.description: Posts on textual variants and scribal changes tied to theological controversy, including disputes over Christology, Gnostic interpretations, and emerging proto-orthodox claims.

topics.name: Translation Process
topics.description: Posts on the process of translating biblical and ancient texts, including word choices, ancient languages, inclusive language, difficult passages, and how translation affects interpretation.

topics.name: Trial of Jesus
topics.description: Posts on Jesus' trial before Jewish leaders or Pilate, including the charge against him, Gospel portrayals, anti-Judaism, Pilate traditions, and later non-canonical expansions.

topics.name: Trinity
topics.description: Posts on the development of the doctrine of the Trinity, including the Father, Son, Spirit, Nicaea, biblical interpretation, and later Christian theology.

topics.name: Triumph of Christianity
topics.description: Posts about how Christianity spread, converted the Roman world, and became a dominant religion.

topics.name: Valentinian Gnostics
topics.description: Posts on Valentinian Christians, their interpretation of scripture, theology, Gospel of Philip, and disputes with other early Christian groups.

topics.name: Virgin Birth
topics.description: Posts on the virgin birth traditions, including Matthew and Luke, textual questions, adoptionist Christology, ancient parallels, and historical or theological interpretation.

topics.name: Visions
topics.description: Posts on visions, group visions, hallucinations, resurrection appearances, and questions about whether visionary experiences can be shared or historically evaluated.

topics.name: Women in Early Christianity
topics.description: Posts on women's roles, leadership, silence, gender hierarchy, and social status in early Christian texts and communities.

topics.name: Writing Process and Book Publishing
topics.description: Posts about writing habits, reading and research methods for books, trade-book production, publishing, titles, agents, reviews, and bringing scholarship to readers.

topics.name: Zealot Hypothesis
topics.description: Posts on claims that Jesus supported armed resistance to Rome, including the temple incident, zealot interpretations, Roman rule, and Bart's critique of the hypothesis.
